business_ideas.idea: SMS text service sending MFM listeners post-episode voice summaries and whiteboard ideas

predictions.text: Personality testing and assessment is massive profitable business with questionable scientific basis
Young math prodigy founders like Scott Wu represent emerging elite talent generation

dynamics: Shaan drives extended research narratives and storytelling; Sam provides skeptical grounding questions. Shaan deeply enthusiastic about Cognition Labs story and personality test business models. Sam more skeptical of claims and limitations. Later collaborate on speedrunning vs walk-backwards debate with alignment that both strategies win.
frameworks: Grit framework - Ability × Zeal × Capacity for labor, or Grit = Zeal × Perseverance
Forer Effect - psychological phenomenon where vague statements feel personally tailored
Walk backwards - willingness to reinvent, restart at zero, reaccept beginner status
Speedrunning - mastering same domain repeatedly, compounding expertise each iteration
Myers-Briggs Type Indicator - 16 personality types, discussed as profitable business model
learnings: Personality and grit tests exploit confirmation bias despite shallow scientific foundation
Test companies create recurring revenue through practitioner certification MLM-style business
Mental and psychological traits differentiate elite performers more than measurable metrics
Top performers often willing to completely reset and lose everything financially to reinvent
Speedrunning mastery of single domain repeatedly can match success of exploring new frontiers
Neither strategic approach objectively superior - outcome depends on personal drive and preference

banger_quotes.quote: I scored 2.5 on this grit test, I'm in the bottom 10 percentile
There's a lot of hand-waving stuff here, this is bullshit
It took longer to read explanation than him to actually answer it
You need to shut the fuck up and go sell some bars

numbers_dropped: Myers-Briggs test: $15-40 per test, $2000 per practitioner certification
MBTI enrolls 5000 practitioners yearly generating $8-10M, total revenue $20M+ (private)
89 of Fortune 100 companies use Myers-Briggs, EPA uses on 25% of 20k employees
Cognition Labs raised $21M with 10 employees holding 10 gold coding competition medals
RxBar sold $600M to Kellogg's; growth: Y1=$2M, Y2=$6M, Y3=$36M, Y4=$161M
E-commerce speedrunner: $500M sale, new company targeting $1M/month in 3 months, hitting $2-3M
E-commerce speedrunner 3: $10M annual profit, no external capital, <5 years old
David Bar macros: 160 calories, 28g protein, sugar-free
notable: Shaan scores only 2.5/5 on Grit test despite evident success, finding contradiction humbling. Celebrates Cognition Labs/Devin as most inspiring recent story. Includes subplot about Scott Wu (CPO dropout from Harvard) whose mother still unaware of his success due to leaving school.
